data.title: Solartech Issues Profit Warning with Increased Losses Expected
data.description:
Solartech International Holdings Limited ( (HK:1166) ) has provided an announcement.

Solartech International Holdings Limited has issued a profit warning, indicating an expected increase in losses for the fiscal year ending June 30, 2025, with losses projected between HK$140 million and HK$148 million, compared to HK$88.5 million the previous year. The increase in losses is attributed to decreased turnover and gross profit, impairment loss on mining rights, and reduced deferred tax credits, despite a one-off gain from a subsidiary disposal and decreased losses from investment property valuations.

data.description:

The outcome of this trial will support the company’s future registration application with the National Medical Products Administration, potentially enhancing its market position in the medical technology industry.

data.title: China Resources Beverage Proposes Domicile Change to Hong Kong
data.description:
China Resources Beverage (Holdings) Company Limited ( (HK:2460) ) has issued an announcement.

China Resources Beverage (Holdings) Company Limited has announced a proposal to change its domicile from the Cayman Islands to Hong Kong. This move is in line with the new company re-domiciliation regime introduced by the Hong Kong government, which aims to simplify compliance and reduce costs for non-Hong Kong corporations. The change will not affect the company’s assets, management, or shareholder interests, and the company will maintain its listing status on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The re-domiciliation is expected to provide a more stable and predictable business environment, benefiting from Hong Kong’s strong rule of law and efficient governance.

data.title: A Metaverse Company Faces Winding-Up Petition Amid Financial Challenges
data.description:

A Metaverse Company is facing a winding-up petition filed by BEITAI INVESTMENT LP due to an outstanding debt of approximately HK$33.4 million from a subscription agreement for unsecured bonds. The petition, scheduled for a court hearing on 26 November 2025, could lead to significant operational and financial implications, including restrictions on share transfers and potential suspension of services by the Hong Kong Securities Clearing Company Limited. The appointed provisional liquidators are currently focused on securing the company’s assets and exploring options regarding the petition.

data.title: Hevol Services Group Announces Board Restructuring
data.description:
Hevol Services Group Co. Limited ( (HK:6093) ) has provided an update.

Hevol Services Group Co. Limited has announced changes in its board composition, reflecting a strategic shift in its governance structure. Dr. Li Yongrui has resigned from his roles as an independent non-executive director and chairman of both the Nomination and Remuneration Committees to focus on other business commitments. In his place, Dr. Chen Lei will serve as the chairman of the Remuneration Committee, Mr. Qian Hongji will take over as chairman of the Nomination Committee, and Ms. Hu Hongfang will join the Remuneration Committee as a member. These changes are effective from September 26, 2025, and are expected to bring fresh perspectives to the company’s board operations.
